desc:
Validation Scope

Validate [PSS]Mailbox Sweep on NWP silicon/emulation.

HAS Ref — Mailbox Sweep: Systematic sweep of PCode mailbox commands (PMSB mailbox interface) to verify correct response for all supported command IDs. Validation: each valid mailbox command returns expected data and status code; unsupported commands return defined error code (not hang or MCA); concurrent mailbox access from multiple agents handled correctly. Interface: BIOS/OS mailbox registers, PMSB command/response protocol.
Preconditions

ItemRequirement
Feature[PSS]Mailbox Sweep
PlatformNWP validation target (silicon or emulation) with PM integration features enabled.
Test Steps

#ActionExpected Result (PASS)
1Identify all supported PCode mailbox command IDs from HAS. Build sweep list.Command ID list populated; no undefined IDs in sweep.
2Issue each mailbox command in sequence via PMSB mailbox interface. Record command, response data, and status code.Each valid command returns expected status (non-error); response data within spec range.
3Issue an unsupported/reserved mailbox command ID. Verify error handling.Unsupported command returns defined error code; no hang or MCA.
4Issue concurrent mailbox commands from multiple agents. Verify serialisation and correct responses.Commands serialised correctly; responses accurate; no deadlock or timeout.
5Run the automation script.Automation PASS; mailbox sweep complete.
Health Check - Registers and Logs

  • No BIOS hang, MCA, or soft hang during or after test execution.
  • No error-level events in NLOG / fw trace.
  • NLOG records invalid-parameter event with expected error code.
  • System remains stable and PM-functional after each negative test.
  • All valid mailbox commands return non-error status; response data within spec.
  • Reserved/invalid command IDs return defined error code; no hang.
Post-Process

Mark PASS when the feature flow completes without instability, all register and telemetry checks pass, and no MCA, hang, or unexpected PM event is observed. Mark FAIL on any MCA, soft hang, register mismatch, unexpected throttle state, or NLOG error-level event.

HAS Reference

  • See Validation Scope for spec / bug references.

tc_title: [TPMI/PMT] Verify Aggregate Margin to Tcontrol PMT Register
desc:
Flow *

CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal10Aggregate Margin to TcontrolPMT


MARGIN_TO_TCONTROL = (EFFECTIVE_TJ_MAX - FUSED_T_CONTROL_OFFSET + DTS_CONFIG3(TCONTROL_OFFSET)) - MAX_TEMPERATURE


Where:

EFFECTIVE_TJ_MAX calculation is detailed in the following TC:

https://hsdes.intel.com/appstore/article/#/14019959390


PKG_MAX_TEMEPRATURE is the Hottest temperature in the Package, max temperature across all the dies:

sv.sockets.cbbs.base.punit_regs.punit_gpsb.gpsb_infvnn_crs.package_temperature

sv.sockets.imhs.uncore.pm_gen4_punit0.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_temperature


FUSED_T_CONTROL_OFFSET is calculated as IF(SST_PP_CONTROL.FEATURE_STATE[SST_PP_LEVEL][0]) SST_BF_CONFIG_TCONTROL_OFFSET[SST_PP_LEVEL] ELSE TCONTROL_OFFSET (Global fuse note that this is not per PP level):

sv.sockets.imhs.fuses.punit.showsearch('pcode_sst_bf_config_.*_t_control_offset')

sv.sockets.imhs.fuses.punit.pcode_t_control_offset


Where the TPMI register values will be used to determine the SST_PP_LEVEL as well as SST_BF configuration.


Obtain the values from the listed sources and verify that, following the calculation indicated in bold, the calculated margin to TControl matches the value reflected through the PMT Mailbox.

** PMT Mailbox command pending

Configuration *

A QDF that supports SST-PP and SST-BF is necessary.


Pass/Fail criteria *

Value of margin to TControl equals the difference between Package Max Temperature and Effective TJMax, accounting for SST and Tcontrol offsets according to configuration.


Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics 0p8.


HW Requirements

N/A


SW Requirements

Pcode, Acode, Patch23.


Coverage

N/A


Component Interactions

N/A

Flow *

CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal2Package TemperaturePMT


This register reflects the temperature margin to throttle, a delta between maximum temperature and TJ max. Individual dies calculate their own margins and send it to the root IMH via HPM message SOCKET_THERMAL. The root Primecode calculates the maximum temperature across does the dies and populates this register.

(HPM HAS): https://docs.intel.com/documents/pm_doc/src/DMR_CBB/HAS/HPM/hpm.html


max(sv.sockets.cbbs.base.punit_regs.punit_gpsb.gpsb_infvnn_crs.package_temperature,sv.sockets.imhs.uncore.pm_gen4_punit0.ptpcioregs.ptpcioregs.package_temperature)


Configuration *

No specific configuration identified as requirement.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Verify that the value for Package temperature listed by TPMI equals the maximum overall margin to TJMax reported by present dies.

Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics.

HW Requirements

N/A

SW Requirements

Acode, Pcode, Patch23.

Coverage

N/A

Component Interactions

N/A

tc_title: [TPMI/PMT] Verify Package Thermal Status TPMI Register
desc:
Flow *

 
CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal20Package Thermal statusTPMI


NamePACKAGE_THERM_STATUS

Size
32
ScopePunit(Package Scoped & Pkg Multi)
DescriptionThermal Status Register (MSR 0X1B1, PCU CR 0xfb984 & IO register)
BitsAccessDefaultDescription
0:0RO/V0x0

THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS

Status bit indicating that the core is currently thermally throttling.
1:1RO/V0x0

THERMAL_MONITOR_LOG

Sticky log bit indicating that the core has seen a thermal monitor event since the last time SW

cleared this bit. Set by HW on a 0 to 1 transition of Thermal_Monitor_Status
2:2RO/V0x0

PROCHOT_STATUS

Status bit indicating that xxPROCHOT# is currently being asserted. Status bit indicating that the state

of the inbound path of the xxPROCHOT# pin.
3:3RO/V0x0

PROCHOT_LOG

Sticky log bit indicating that xxPROCHOT# has been asserted since the last time this bit was cleared

by SW. Set by HW on a 0 to 1 transition of Prochot_Status
4:4RO/V0x0

OUT_OF_SPEC_STATUS

Status bit indicating that the processor is operating out of its thermal specification. It is set when

maximum throttling is engaged for a certain amount of time. It should be cleard if running below

the throttling temperature.
5:5RO/V0x0

OUT_OF_SPEC_LOG

Sticky log bit indicating that the processor operating out of its thermal specification since the last

time this bit was cleared. Set by HW on a 0 to 1 transition of Out_of_Spec_Status
6:6RO/V0x0

THRESHOLD1_STATUS

Indicates that the current temperature (bits 12:16) is higher than or equal to the Threshold1 defined

in the IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_INTERRUPT MSR (aka IO_Package_therm_interrupt). Note that

because temperature and thresholds are defined as negative offsets, a higher number means a

lower temperature.
7:7RO/V0x0

THRESHOLD1_LOG

Sticky log bit that asserts on a 0 to 1 or 1 to 0 transition of the Threshold1_Status bit. HW controls

this transition. Ucode is capable of overwriting the data.
8:8RO/V0x0

THRESHOLD2_STATUS

Indicates that the current temperature (bits 22:16) is higher than or equal to the Threshold2 defined

in the IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_INTERRUPT MSR (aka IO_Package_therm_interrupt). Note tha

because temperature and thresholds are defined as negative offsets, a higher number mea

lower temperature.
9:9RO/V0x0

THRESHOLD2_LOG

Sticky log bit that asserts on a 0 to 1 or 1 to 0 transition of the Threshold2_Status bit. HW controls

this transition. Ucode is capable of overwriting the data.
10:10RO/V0x0

POWER_LIMITATION_STATUS

Status log bit that notify if PP P state is below the max P state offset
11:11RO/V0x0

POWER_LIMITATION_LOG

power limitation log bit
12:12RO/V0x0

PMAX_STATUS

Status bit for the PMAX detector. An assertion means the PMAX detector circuit has asserted(upto a 1ms delay). The status bit is deasserted when the core throttle is released to indicate the condition has been dealt with.
13:13RO/V0x0

PMAX_LOG

Sticky log bit indicating the PMAX detector has been asserted since the last time this bit was cleared by SW. Set by HW on a 0 to 1 transition of PMAX_STATUS.
23:16RO/V0x0

TEMPERATURE

Temperature in degrees C, relative to the thermal monitor trip temperature (fused)
26:26RO/V0x0

HW_FEEDBACK_NOTIFICATION_LOG

Sticky log bit that indicates whether a HW FEEBACK Change has occurred (e.g. due to power/thermal limits) since the last clearing of this bit or reset. SW may clear this bit by writing a zero (0).
30:27RO/V0x1

RESOLUTION

Supported resolution in degrees C. Hard-coded to '0001. No Flops here
31:31RO/V0x0

VALID

Set if temperature is within the valid thermal sensor range
<> Temperature:
TEMPERATURE = PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE - EFFECTIVE_TJ_MAX

<> Thermal Monitor:

Set Status bit whenever Package temperature reaches or exceeds junction temperature:

if ( PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE >= EFFECTIVE_TJ_MAX ) 
    RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = 1 
else 
    RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = 0

Accounting for the delay set by the programmable TAU if the feature is enabled:

ALPHA =exp^(-1 * DELTA_TIME /TAU)
EWMA_FILTERED_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = ((1-ALPHA) * PREV_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS) + (ALPHA * CURRENT_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS )

<> Thresholds 1 and 2:

Status bit should assert according to current package temperature and offset value for the given threshold as set in MSR 0x1B2 (IA32_PACKAGE_THERM_INTERRUPT ):

if ((EFFECTIVE_TJMAX - PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE) <= PACKAGE_THERM_INTERRUPT[THRESHOLDX_REL_TEMP]))
    THRESHOLDX_STATUS = 1  
else 
    THRESHOLDX_STATUS = 0

<> Out of spec:

OOS status bit gets set whenever the Junction temperature is surpassed by 10 C or the dies has been operating at max throttle for a given amount of time.

IF (PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE >=  EFFECTIVE_TJMAX + THERM_OUTOFSPEC_TEMP )
    
    OUT_OF_SPEC = 1

ELSE IF(GLOBAL_THERMAL_OUTSPEC_TIMER_EXPIRED )

    OUT_OF_SPEC = 1

ELSE IF(PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE <  EFFECTIVE_TJMAX)

    OUT_OF_SPEC = 0


where THERM_OUTOFSPEC_TEMP is 10 degree C

<> Prochot:

Set on the rising or falling edge of xxPROCHOT_N.


Configuration *

Make sure thermal interruptions are enabled, test with both Thermal Monitor Enabled and Disabled, have SST-PP fuse enabled.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Validate that each field behaves according to the expectations set by the description of each field, ie. Temperature value equals the margin to effTjMax, Thermal monitor status bit asserts at and above the Junction temperature, etc.

Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics.

HW Requirements

N/A

SW Requirements

Pcode, Acode, Patch23.

Coverage

N/A

Component Interactions

N/A

Flow *

 
CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal16Temperature TargetPMT


Check that the values on the following three fields match the corresponding fused value: 

  • TJ_MAX_TCC_OFFSET this can be programmed by customers and defaults to 0. 
  • FAN_TEMP_TARGET_OFFSET (15:8) = The FAN_TEMP_TARGET_OFST is the relative offset to the thermal monitor trip temperature at which fans engage and programmed by the Primecode as per the resolved fuse(FUSED_T_CONTROL_OFFSET).
  • REF_TEMP (23:16) = EFFECTIVE_TJMAX - FUSE_DTS_CAL_GUARDBAND

\

**Need to confirm requirement for a config with DTS_CONFIG3 disabled: Diamond Rapids Server (DMR) SoC Thermal Management HAS (intel.com)


Configuration *

No particular configuration identified.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Reported values match the fused counterpart and are honored for their respective uses, ei. Margin to TControl.

Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics.

HW Requirements

N/A

SW Requirements

Acode, Pcode, Patch23.

Coverage

N/A

Component Interactions

N/A

tc_title: [TPMI/PMT] Verify Thermal Constrained Time PMT Register
desc:
(Sections with * are mandatory. You must fill them in, or say "Not Applicable" or "N/A" if you have seriously considered it and determined there's nothing to say. Remove these instructions and the explanatory text in each section as you fill things in.)

Flow *

 
CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal32Thermal constrained timePMT



https://docs.intel.com/documents/primecode/has/PMT_Definitions/dmr_imh/pmt_telemetry.html#package_temperature


1. Take note of current value of the counter.

2. Override temperature through DTS or DFX past Junction Temperature.

3. Counter should increase, one as long as thermal conditions are in place, the other as long as domain ratio is below P1 (guaranteed ratio).

4. Disengage temperature override.

5. Take note of the value of the counters two more times.



Configuration *

EMTTM Enabled.

Pass/Fail criteria *

Counters should increase as time transpire with thermal conditions throttling the system. Once released, the increment should halt.

Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics.

HW Requirements

N/A

SW Requirements

Acode, Pcode, Patch23.

Coverage

N/A

Component Interactions

N/A

tc_title: [TPMI/PMT] Verify Thermal Monitor Filtering TPMI Register
desc:
Flow *

 
CategoryPCS IndexPCS descriptionDMR Support
Package Thermal20Controlling the Thermal monitor filteringTPMI


An extension of the test done for: 14019957652 - [TPMI/PMT] Verify Package Thermal Status TPMI Register (intel.com).

Verify that the time delay derived from the Tau value set int this TPMI register appropriately delays the assertion of the Thermal Monitor Status bit. Following the calculation shown below:

if ( PKG_MAX_TEMPERATURE >= EFFECTIVE_TJ_MAX ) 
    RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = 1 
else 
    RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = 0


CURRENT_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS 

ALPHA =exp^(-1 * DELTA_TIME /TAU)
EWMA_FILTERED_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = ((1-ALPHA) * PREV_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS) + (ALPHA * CURRENT_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS ) 

PREV_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = CURRENT_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS

if(THERMAL_MONITOR_FILTER_ENABLE = 1)
    THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = EWMA_FILTERED_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS

else
    THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS = RAW_THERMAL_MONITOR_STATUS


where:
DELTA_TIME is ELAPSED time from previous sample
TAU is time window programmable via BIOS
1. After booting with TMF enabled.

2. Change the default value of Tau to a value that would allow us to measure the time delay.

3. Rise temperature to effTJMax through DTS or DFX override.

4. Verify that status bit is asserted after the calculated delay time has elapsed.


Configuration *

Enable Thermal Monitor Filtering through BIOS Knobs.

Pass/Fail criteria *

After reaching/exceeding the Junction temperature, the Thermal Monitor Status bit should assert after the time frame (configurable through Tau on the TPMI field).

Intent Verification Strategy *

Simics.

HW Requirements

N/A

SW Requirements

Acode, Pcode, Patch23.

Coverage

N/A

Component Interactions

N/A
